info.title: TiDB Cloud Premium API
info.description:
*TiDB Cloud API is in beta.*

This API manages [TiDB Cloud Premium](https://docs.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/select-cluster-tier/#tidb-cloud-premium) instances. For more information about TiDB Cloud API, see [TiDB Cloud API Overview](https://docs.pingcap.com/api/tidb-cloud-api-overview/).

# Overview

The TiDB Cloud Premium API (v1beta2) provides [REST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/REST) endpoints to manage TiDB Cloud Premium instances and related resources.

You can use this API to manage the following resources:

- **TiDB Cloud Premium instance**: manage the lifecycle and configuration of TiDB Cloud Premium instances, including passwords, CA certificates, and cloud provider information.
- **Backup**: manage backups for TiDB Cloud Premium instances, including backup-based restore.
- **Region**: retrieve available regions for deploying TiDB Cloud Premium instances.

# Get Started

This guide helps you make your first API call to the TiDB Cloud Premium API. You will learn how to authenticate a request, build a request, and interpret the response.

1. Create a [TiDB Cloud account](https://tidbcloud.com/signup) if you do not already have one.
2. In the [TiDB Cloud console](https://tidbcloud.com/), go to **Organization** > **API Keys** and create an API key. For more information, see [API key management](#section/Authentication/API-key-management).
3. Make your first API call.

	To get all TiDB Cloud Premium instances in your organization, run the following command in your terminal. Replace `YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY` and `YOUR_PRIVATE_KEY` with your own key values.

	```bash
	curl --digest \
	  --user 'YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY:YOUR_PRIVATE_KEY' \
	  --request GET \
	  --url 'https://cloud.tidbapi.com/v1beta2/tidbs' \
	  --header 'Accept: application/json'
	```

4. The API returns a JSON list of your TiDB Cloud Premium instances. If none exist, the response contains an empty list.

# Authentication

The TiDB Cloud API uses [HTTP Digest Authentication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digest_access_authentication). It protects your private key from being sent over the network. For more details about HTTP Digest Authentication, refer to the [IETF RFC](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7616).

## API key overview

- The API key contains a public key and a private key, which act as the username and password required in the HTTP Digest Authentication. The private key only displays upon the key creation.
- The API key belongs to your organization and acts as the `Organization Owner` role. You can check [permissions of owner](https://docs.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/manage-user-access#configure-member-roles).
- You must provide the correct API key in every request. Otherwise, TiDB Cloud responds with a `401` error.

## API key management

### Create an API key

Only the **owner** of an organization can create an API key.

To create an API key in an organization, perform the following steps:

1. In the [TiDB Cloud console](https://tidbcloud.com), switch to your target organization using the combo box in the upper-left corner.
2. In the left navigation pane, click **Organization Settings** > **API Keys**.
3. On the **API Keys** page, click **Create API Key**.
4. Enter a description for your API key. The role of the API key is always `Organization Owner` currently.
5. Click **Next**. Copy and save the public key and the private key.
6. Make sure that you have copied and saved the private key in a secure location. The private key only displays upon the creation. After leaving this page, you will not be able to get the full private key again.
7. Click **Done**.

### View details of an API key

To view details of an API key, perform the following steps:

1. In the [TiDB Cloud console](https://tidbcloud.com), switch to your target organization using the combo box in the upper-left corner.
2. In the left navigation pane, click **Organization Settings** > **API Keys**.
3. You can view the details of the API keys on the page.

### Edit an API key

Only the **owner** of an organization can modify an API key.

To edit an API key in an organization, perform the following steps:

1. In the [TiDB Cloud console](https://tidbcloud.com), switch to your target organization using the combo box in the upper-left corner.
2. In the left navigation pane, click **Organization Settings** > **API Keys**.
3. On the **API Keys** page, click **...** in the API key row that you want to change, and then click **Edit**.
4. You can update the API key description.
5. Click **Update**.

### Delete an API key

Only the **owner** of an organization can delete an API key.

To delete an API key in an organization, perform the following steps:

1. In the [TiDB Cloud console](https://tidbcloud.com), switch to your target organization using the combo box in the upper-left corner.
2. In the left navigation pane, click **Organization Settings** > **API Keys**.
3. On the **API Keys** page, click **...** in the API key row that you want to delete, and then click **Delete**.
4. Click **I understand, delete it.**

# API Changelog

This changelog lists all changes to the TiDB Cloud Premium API (v1beta2).

<!-- In reverse chronological order -->

## 20260331

- Initial release of the TiDB Cloud Premium API (v1beta2), including the following resources and endpoints:
	* TiDB Cloud Premium instance
		* [List TiDB Cloud Premium instances](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_ListTidbs)
		* [Create a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_CreateTidb)
		* [Get a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_GetTidb)
		* [Delete a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_DeleteTidb)
		* [Update a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_UpdateTidb)
		* [Reset the root password of a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_ResetRootPassword)
		* [Get cloud provider information for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_GetCloudProviderInfo)
		* [Get the CA certificate download URL for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_GetCaCertificateDownloadUrl)
	* Backup
		* [List backups for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/Backup/operation/TidbService_ListTidbBackups)
		* [Delete a backup for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/Backup/operation/TidbService_DeleteTidbBackup)
		* [Restore a TiDB Cloud Premium instance from a backup](#tag/Backup/operation/TidbService_RestoreTidb)
		* [Get restore status for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/Backup/operation/TidbService_GetRestoreStatus)
	* Region
		* [List regions](#tag/Region/operation/RegionService_ListRegions)

tags.name: TiDB Cloud Premium Instance
tags.description: Manage the lifecycle and configuration of TiDB Cloud Premium instances, including passwords, CA certificates, and cloud provider information.

tags.name: Backup
tags.description: Manage backups for TiDB Cloud Premium instances, including backup-based restore.

tags.name: Region
tags.description: Retrieve available regions for deploying TiDB Cloud Premium instances.

get.description: Lists all TiDB Cloud Premium instances in your organization. You can filter results by service plan, region, name, or ID.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: servicePlan
parameters.description:
The plan of the service. If specified, only TiDB Cloud Premium instances of the specified plan are returned.

- `Premium`: [TiDB Cloud Premium](https://docs.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/select-cluster-tier/#tidb-cloud-premium)

parameters.name: regionIds
parameters.description: A list of region IDs. If specified, only TiDB Cloud Premium instances in these regions are returned.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The display name to filter by. If specified, only TiDB Cloud Premium instances whose display name contains this value are returned.

parameters.name: tidbIds
parameters.description: A list of TiDB Cloud Premium instance IDs. If specified, only the ones matching these IDs are returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The maximum number of TiDB Cloud Premium instances to return. If not specified, at most 10 will be returned. The maximum value is `100`. Values greater than `100` are set to `100`.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
The pagination token received from a previous [List TiDB Cloud Premium instances](#tag/TiDB-Instance/operation/TidbService_ListTidbs) request. Use this token to retrieve the next page of results.

**Note**: When paginating, all other parameters must match the original request.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of TiDB Cloud Premium instances to skip before returning results. If the value exceeds the total number of TiDB Cloud Premium instances, the response is `200` with an empty list and no `nextPageToken`.

post.summary: Create a TiDB Cloud Premium instance
post.description: Creates a new TiDB Cloud Premium instance. If the `tidb.cloud/project` label is provided, the instance will be created in the specified project.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidb
parameters.description: The TiDB Cloud Premium instance to create.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed. Defaults to `false`.

parameters.description: If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed. Defaults to `false`.

patch.summary: Update a TiDB Cloud Premium instance
patch.description: Updates the configuration of a specific TiDB Cloud Premium instance. You can modify the following fields: `displayName` and `maxRcu`.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidb.tidbId
parameters.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance to update.

parameters.name: tidb
parameters.description: The TiDB Cloud Premium instance to update.

displayName.description: The user-defined name of the cluster.

minRcu.description:
Deprecated: min_rcu is now automatically managed.
Any value provided here will be ignored in favor of max(5000, max_rcu / 4).

maxRcu.description: The maximum number of Request Capacity Units (RCUs) for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

schema.title: The TiDB Cloud Premium instance to update.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed. Defaults to `false`.

post.summary: Reset the root password of a TiDB Cloud Premium instance
post.description: Sets a new password for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance's root user. The new password is not returned in the response for security reasons, so save your password in a secure location.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidbId
parameters.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance for which to reset the root password.

get.summary: Get cloud provider information for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance
get.description: Retrieves the TiDB Cloud account ID and external ID for the underlying cloud infrastructure of a specific TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidbId
parameters.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance for which to retrieve cloud provider information.

get.summary: List backups for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance
get.description: Lists all backups for a specific TiDB Cloud Premium instance. You can filter results by state, region, trigger type, or time range.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidbId
parameters.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance for which to list backups.

parameters.name: states
parameters.description:
A list of backup states. If specified, only backups matching these states are returned.

 - UNKNOWN: The backup state is unknown.
 - PENDING: The backup is pending.
 - RUNNING: The backup is in progress.
 - SUCCEEDED: The backup completed successfully.
 - FAILED: The backup has failed.
 - CANCELLED: The backup is cancelled.

parameters.name: regionIds
parameters.description: A list of region IDs. If specified, only backups in these regions are returned.

parameters.name: triggerTypes
parameters.description:
A list of backup trigger types. If specified, only backups matching these trigger types are returned.

 - AUTO: The backup is triggered automatically by TiDB Cloud.
 - MANUAL: The backup is triggered manually by a user.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: The inclusive start of the backup time range, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format. If specified, only backups at or after this time are returned.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: The inclusive end of the backup time range, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format. If specified, only backups at or before this time are returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The maximum number of backups to return. If not specified, at most 10 backups will be returned. The maximum value is `100`. Values greater than `100` are set to `100`.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
The pagination token received from a previous [List backups for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance](#tag/Backup/operation/TidbService_ListTidbBackups) request. Use this token to retrieve the next page of results.

**Note**: When paginating, all other parameters must match the original request.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of backups to skip before returning results. If the value exceeds the total number of backups, the response is `200` with an empty list and no `nextPageToken`.

delete.summary: Delete a backup for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidbId
parameters.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

parameters.name: backupId
parameters.description: The ID of the backup to delete.

post.summary: Restore a TiDB Cloud Premium instance from a backup
post.description: Restores a new TiDB Cloud Premium instance from a backup. Supported restore modes: `SNAPSHOT`, `PITR`, `DEDICATED_SNAPSHOT`, and `CLASSIC_SNAPSHOT`.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

get.summary: Get the restore state for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance
get.description: Retrieves the restore progress of a TiDB Cloud Premium instance, including the completion percentage and any error messages.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidbId
parameters.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

get.summary: Get the CA certificate download URL for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance
get.description: Retrieves the download URL for the CA certificate required to establish TLS (Transport Layer Security) connections to a specific TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: tidbId
parameters.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance for which to retrieve the CA certificate.

get.description: Lists the regions where you can create a TiDB Cloud Premium instance. You can filter results by cloud provider or service plan.

401.description: The API key cannot be authenticated.

403.description: The API key does not have permission to access the resource.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit.

parameters.name: cloudProvider
parameters.description: The cloud provider. If specified, only regions belonging to the specified cloud provider are returned.

parameters.name: servicePlan
parameters.description: The plan of the service. If specified,  only regions that support the specified service plan are returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The maximum number of regions to return. If not specified, at most 10 regions will be returned. The maximum value is `100`. Values greater than `100` are set to `100`.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
The pagination token received from a previous [List regions](#tag/Region/operation/RegionService_ListRegions) request. Use this token to retrieve the next page of results.

**Note**: When paginating, all other parameters must match the original request.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of regions to skip before returning results. If the value exceeds the total number of regions, the response is `200` with an empty list and no `nextPageToken`.

BackupTriggerType.description:
The trigger type of a backup.

 - AUTO: The backup is triggered automatically by TiDB Cloud.
 - MANUAL: The backup is triggered manually by a user.

ramRoleArn.description: The ARN of the Alibaba Cloud RAM role that accesses the KMS key.

accountId.description: The AWS account ID of the CMEK IAM principal.

serviceActive.description: Indicates whether the service is active.

endpointActive.description: Indicates whether the endpoint is active.

dnsReachable.description: Indicates whether the DNS is reachable.

message.description: A human-readable message that describes the reachability status.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Alibaba Cloud KMS key used for encryption.

kmsInstanceId.description: The ID of the Alibaba Cloud KMS instance that hosts the key.

kmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS KMS key used for encryption.

RegionCloudProvider.description:
Enum of cloud provider names.

 - aws: Amazon Web Services.
 - gcp: Google Cloud Platform.
 - azure: Microsoft Azure.
 - alicloud: Alibaba Cloud

noEncryption.description: Disables dual-layer data encryption. The TiDB Cloud Premium instance uses the default encryption provided by the cloud provider.

defaultKey.description: Enables dual-layer data encryption using the TiDB Cloud default key.

cmek.description:
Enables dual-layer data encryption using a customer-managed encryption key (CMEK).
Configure the key according to the cloud provider:
- AWS: set `awsKms` and `awsPrincipal`.
- Alibaba Cloud: set `aliyunKms` and `aliyunPrincipal`.

TidbDualLayerDataEncryption.description: The dual-layer data encryption configuration for a TiDB Cloud Premium instance. Specify exactly one of the following options: `noEncryption`, `defaultKey`, or `cmek`.

host.description:
The hostname of the endpoint.

When `connectionType` is `"PRIVATE_ENDPOINT"`, the behavior of the `host` field varies by cloud provider:
- For AWS, this field is available after the Private Link service is ready.
- For Google Cloud and Azure, this field is available after you create a private endpoint connection.

port.description: The port number of the endpoint.

connectionType.description:
The connection type of the endpoint.

- `PUBLIC`: a public endpoint. For more information, see [Network](https://docs-preview.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/connect-to-tidb-instance/#network).

- `PRIVATE_ENDPOINT`: a private endpoint. For more information, see [Network](https://docs-preview.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/connect-to-tidb-instance/#network).

- `VPC_PEERING`: a VPC peering endpoint (not yet supported, planned for future).

connectionReachability.description: The reachability status of the endpoint connection.

TidbHighAvailabilityType.description:
The high availability configuration for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

 - REGIONAL: High availability across multiple availability zones within a region.
 - ZONAL: High availability within a single availability zone.

rootPassword.description: The new root password. It must be between 8 and 64 characters long.

name.description: The unique identifier for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance, which is generated by the API and follows the format `tidbs/{tidbId}`.

tidbId.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

displayName.description: The user-defined name of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

regionId.description: The unique identifier of the region where the TiDB Cloud Premium instance is deployed, in the format of `{cloud_provider}-{region_code}`. For example, `aws-us-west-2`.

cloudProvider.description:
The cloud provider where the TiDB Cloud Premium instance is deployed.

- `"aws"`: Amazon Web Services

- `"gcp"`: Google Cloud

- `"azure"`: Microsoft Azure

- `"alicloud"`: Alibaba Cloud

regionDisplayName.description: The display name of the region where the TiDB Cloud Premium instance is deployed. For example, `Oregon (us-west-2)`.

state.description: The current state of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

rootPassword.description: The root password of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance. It must be between 8 and 64 characters long and can contain letters, numbers, and special characters.

minRcu.description:
The minimum number of Request Capacity Units (RCUs) for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

This field is read-only and is automatically calculated as `max(5000, maxRcu / 4)`.

maxRcu.description: The maximum number of Request Capacity Units (RCUs) for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

servicePlan.description:
The plan of the service.

- `Premium`: [TiDB Cloud Premium](https://docs.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/select-cluster-tier/#tidb-cloud-premium)

highAvailabilityType.description:
The high availability configuration for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.
- `REGIONAL`: high availability across multiple availability zones within a region. This is the only supported mode for TiDB Cloud Premium instances. For more information, see [Regional high availability architecture](https://docs.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/serverless-high-availability/#regional-high-availability-architecture).
- `ZONAL`: high availability within a single availability zone. This mode is not supported for the Premium Plan and is reserved for other cloud service tiers. For more information, see [Zonal high availability architecture](https://docs.pingcap.com/tidbcloud/serverless-high-availability/#zonal-high-availability-architecture).

annotations.description:
The annotations for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance. The following lists some predefined annotations:
- `tidb.cloud/has-set-password`: indicates whether the TiDB Cloud Premium instance has a root password set.
- `tidb.cloud/available-features`: lists available features of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

labels.description:
The labels for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.
- `tidb.cloud/organization`: the ID of the organization where the TiDB Cloud Premium instance belongs.
- `tidb.cloud/project`: the ID of the project where the TiDB Cloud Premium instance belongs.

creator.description: The email address or public API key of the user who created the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

createTime.description: The timestamp when the TiDB Cloud Premium instance was created, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

updateTime.description: The timestamp when the TiDB Cloud Premium instance was last updated, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

endpoints.description: The connection endpoints for accessing the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

dualLayerDataEncryption.description: The dual-layer data encryption configuration for the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

tidbVersion.description: The version of the TiDB instance.

@type.description: A URL or resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized protocol buffer message.

code.description: The error code returned with this error.

message.description: The error message returned with this error.

details.description: A list of messages with additional error details.

rpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

v1beta1ClusterState.description:
Enum of possible states of a cluster.

 - CREATING: Cluster is being created.
 - DELETING: Cluster is being deleted.
 - ACTIVE: Cluster is active for use.
 - RESTORING: Cluster data is being restored.
 - MAINTENANCE: Cluster is under maintenance.
 - DELETED: Cluster has been deleted.
 - INACTIVE: Cluster is not active, but not being deleted.
 - UPGRADING: Cluster is being updated.
Only for Dedicated Cluster.
 - IMPORTING: Cluster is being imported.
Only for Dedicated Cluster.
 - MODIFYING: Cluster is being modified.
Only for Dedicated Cluster.
 - PAUSING: Cluster is being paused.
Only for Dedicated Cluster.
 - PAUSED: Cluster is paused.
Only for Dedicated Cluster.
 - RESUMING: Cluster is resuming.
Only for Dedicated Cluster.

name.description: The unique name of the region, in the format of `regions/{region_id}`. For example, `regions/aws-us-west-2`.

regionId.description: The unique identifier for the region, in the format of `{cloud_provider}-{region_code}`. For example, `aws-us-west-2`.

cloudProvider.description:
The cloud provider that offers the region.

- `"aws"`: Amazon Web Services

- `"gcp"`: Google Cloud

- `"azure"`: Microsoft Azure

- `"alicloud"`: Alibaba Cloud

displayName.description: A human-readable name for the region. For example, `Oregon (us-west-2)`.

provider.description: **Deprecated.** Use `cloudProvider` instead. The name of the cloud provider. For example, `aws`, `gcp`, `azure`, or `alicloud`.
provider.title: Deprecated

servicePlans.description: The service plans available in this region.
servicePlans.title: ServicePlan

v1beta1Region.description: A representation of a region for deploying TiDB clusters.

id.description: The unique identifier of the backup.

tidbId.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance to which the backup belongs.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization to which the backup belongs.

servicePlan.description: The service plan of the backup. The possible value is `Premium`.

createTime.description: The timestamp when the backup was created, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

sizeBytes.description: The size of the backup in bytes.

state.description: The current state of the backup.

triggerType.description: The trigger type of the backup.

expirationTime.description: The timestamp when the backup expires, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

regionId.description: The ID of the region where the backup is stored.

customerManaged.description: Whether the source cluster used a customer-managed key at backup time. Combine with encryption_key_id: "0" means not encrypted; non-zero key id with false means SMEK; non-zero key id with true means CMEK.
customerManaged.title:
Whether the source cluster used a customer-managed key at backup time.
Combine with `encryption_key_id`:
- `encryption_key_id == "0"`: backup was not encrypted
- `encryption_key_id != "0" && customer_managed == false`: backup used SMEK
- `encryption_key_id != "0" && customer_managed == true`: backup used CMEK

encryptionKeyId.description: Source cluster encryption key ID at backup time. "0" means no encryption key was used.

v1beta2BackupState.description:
The state of a backup.

 - UNKNOWN: The backup state is unknown.
 - PENDING: The backup is pending.
 - RUNNING: The backup is in progress.
 - SUCCEEDED: The backup completed successfully.
 - FAILED: The backup has failed.
 - CANCELLED: The backup is cancelled.

accessKeyId.description: The access key ID used to access the backup storage.

accessKeySecret.description: The access key secret used to access the backup storage.

v1beta2BackupType.description:
The type of a backup.

 - SNAPSHOT: A full snapshot backup.

uri.description: The download URL for the CA certificate of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

cloudProvider.description:
The cloud provider where the backup object storage is located.

**Note**: Currently, only `"aws"` and `"alicloud"` are supported.

regionId.description:
The region where the backup object storage is located, in the format of `{cloud_provider}-{region_code}`. For example, `alicloud-cn-shanghai`.

This field is required when `cloud_provider` is `"alicloud"`.

bucketUri.description:
The URI of the backup bucket.

- For Amazon S3, use the format `s3://{bucket_name}/{path}`. For example, `s3://my-backup-bucket/tidb-backups/`.

- For Alibaba Cloud OSS, use the format `oss://{bucket_name}/{path}`. For example, `oss://my-backup-bucket/tidb-backups/`.

auth.description: The authentication credentials for accessing the backup storage.

v1beta2ClassicBackupStorage.description: The storage configuration for a classic backup.

tidbId.description: The ID of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

cloudProvider.description:
The cloud provider where the TiDB Cloud Premium instance is deployed.

- `"aws"`: Amazon Web Services

- `"gcp"`: Google Cloud

- `"azure"`: Microsoft Azure

- `"alicloud"`: Alibaba Cloud

tidbCloudAccountId.description: The TiDB Cloud account ID for the underlying cloud infrastructure of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

tidbCloudAccountExternalId.description: The external ID of the TiDB Cloud account, which is used for assuming Identity and Access Management (IAM) roles for cross-account access.

reachable.description: Indicates whether the endpoint is reachable.

detail.description: The detailed reachability status of the endpoint connection.

name.description: The unique name of the CMEK, in the format of `cmeks/{cmek_id}`.

regionId.description: The region where the CMEK is located, in the format of `{cloud_provider}-{region_code}`. For example, `aws-us-west-2`.

cloudProvider.description:
The cloud provider where the CMEK is located.

- `"aws"`: Amazon Web Services

- `"gcp"`: Google Cloud (not yet supported, planned for future)

- `"azure"`: Microsoft Azure (not yet supported, planned for future)

- `"alicloud"`: Alibaba Cloud

aliyunKms.description: The configuration for Alibaba Cloud KMS.

createTime.description: The timestamp when the CMEK was created, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

state.description: The current state of the restore operation.

message.description: The detailed error message if the restore operation fails.

progress.description: The restore progress percentage, from `0` to `100`.

regions.description: A list of regions that match the query.

totalSize.description: The total number of regions that match the query.

nextPageToken.description: The token to retrieve the next page of results. Use this value as the `pageToken` parameter in the next request. This field is empty when there are no more pages.

backups.description: A list of backups that match the query.

totalSize.description: The total number of backups that match the query.

nextPageToken.description: The token to retrieve the next page of results. Use this value as the `pageToken` parameter in the next request. This field is empty when there are no more pages.

tidbs.description: A list of TiDB Cloud Premium instances that match the query.

totalSize.description: The total number of TiDB Cloud Premium instances that match the query.

nextPageToken.description: The token to retrieve the next page of results. Use this value as the `pageToken` parameter in the next request. This field is empty when there are no more pages.

v1beta2RestoreMode.description:
The mode used to restore a TiDB Cloud Premium instance.

 - SNAPSHOT: Restores from native snapshot backup data of a TiDB Cloud Premium instance.
 - PITR: Restores from native point-in-time recovery (PITR) backup data of a TiDB Cloud Premium instance.
 - DEDICATED_SNAPSHOT: Restores from snapshot backup data of a TiDB Cloud Dedicated cluster.
 - CLASSIC_SNAPSHOT: Restores from snapshot backup data stored in object storage of a classic TiDB

v1beta2RestoreState.description:
The current state of the restore operation.

 - UNKNOWN: The state of the restore operation is unknown.
 - PENDING: The restore operation is pending.
 - RUNNING: The restore operation is in progress.
 - SUCCEEDED: The restore operation completed successfully.
 - FAILED: The restore operation has failed.
 - CANCELLED: The restore operation is cancelled.

tidb.description:
The configuration of the TiDB Cloud Premium instance to create.

**Note**: Currently, only TiDB Cloud Premium instances with `servicePlan` set to `Premium` are supported.

sourceTidbId.description: The ID of the source TiDB Cloud Premium instance containing the backup data.

backupId.description:
The ID of the backup to restore from.

This field is required when `restoreMode` is `"SNAPSHOT"` or `"DEDICATED_SNAPSHOT"`.

pointInTime.description:
The point in time to restore data to, in the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

This field is required when `restoreMode` is `"PITR"`.

classicBackupStorage.description:
The classic backup storage configuration.

This field is required when `restoreMode` is `"CLASSIC_SNAPSHOT"`.

validateOnly.description: If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed. Defaults to `false`.

tidb.description: The restored TiDB Cloud Premium instance.
